summary: Comprehensive backend testing of FocusGuard AI completed. 22/22 pytest tests passed (100%). Covered public health, auth (register/login/logout/refresh/me, httpOnly cookies), sessions (create/list/stats), settings (get/update/test-webhook), AI recommendations (Claude Sonnet 4.5), and admin endpoints (users/sessions/health/n8n-status, RBAC). Verified _id and password_hash never leak in responses, bcrypt is in use (login works), and httpOnly cookies are set on register/login.

minor.issue: Cookies are deleted with default attributes (no samesite=none/secure). They were set with samesite=none/secure, so some browsers may not match the delete cookie directive. Recommend matching attributes when calling response.delete_cookie.

Backend CORS allow_origins uses FRONTEND_URL from backend/.env which differs from frontend's REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL host. FRONTEND_URL is 'https://d242dff1-...preview.emergentagent.com' but the actual frontend is served from 'https://blink-monitor-ai.preview.emergentagent.com'. Browser CORS preflight from the real frontend origin will fail. Update FRONTEND_URL in backend/.env to match the deployed frontend URL or use CORS_ORIGINS env variable.
Cookie set during refresh hardcodes secure=true & samesite=none — fine for prod HTTPS but inconsistent with set_auth_cookies helper (could be DRY-extracted).

action_items: Fix CORS: backend/.env FRONTEND_URL ('https://d242dff1-fb21-4c00-a1b5-f5b69188b4e8.preview.emergentagent.com') does NOT match the live frontend host ('https://blink-monitor-ai.preview.emergentagent.com' from frontend/.env REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL). Browser requests with credentials will fail CORS preflight. Update FRONTEND_URL or read CORS_ORIGINS env (already set to '*', but '*' is incompatible with allow_credentials=True).
On /api/auth/logout, pass samesite='none', secure=True, path='/' to response.delete_cookie so the Set-Cookie deletion directive matches the original cookie attributes across browsers.
critical_code_review_comments: CORS misconfiguration: allow_origins reads FRONTEND_URL (single host) but the actual frontend host (REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL in frontend/.env) is different. Note that backend/.env has CORS_ORIGINS='*' but it is unused; if used with allow_credentials=True it would also fail per spec.
@app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown') is deprecated in FastAPI; consider lifespan context manager.
AI recommendations endpoint catches all exceptions and returns 200 with fallback — good for UX but masks real failures from monitoring; consider logging metrics.
server.py is ~600 lines and growing — consider splitting into routers (auth.py, sessions.py, settings.py, admin.py, ai.py) per the >700 line guideline as the app expands.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@focusguard.ai / Admin@FocusGuard123 (works). Test users created dynamically with email pattern test_<ts>_<rand>@focusguard.ai / TestUser@123.
seed_data_creation: Test creates ephemeral users with TEST_-style timestamped emails for isolation. No cleanup performed (data accumulates in focusguard_ai DB but is harmless and uniquely-named).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend at https://blink-monitor-ai.preview.emergentagent.com is fully functional. All 22 backend tests pass. Re-run with: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_focusguard_backend.py -v. Auth uses httpOnly cookies (access_token + refresh_token, samesite=none, secure=true). Use a requests.Session() cookie jar. The only non-functional issue is CORS configuration mismatch which would only manifest from browser (not from server-to-server tests).
